expectations.text: The draft identifies the request as a bug ticket and uses the bug template headings: Description, Steps to Reproduce, Actual Behavior, Expected Behavior, and Additional Information.
expectations.evidence: Draft title describes a bug; body includes ## Description, ## Steps to Reproduce, ## Actual Behavior, ## Expected Behavior, ## Additional Information (lines 32–68).

expectations.text: The response references the notifications implementation with concrete repo context such as NotificationsViewModel, NotificationsOrchestrator, NotificationRepository, or DefaultNotificationRepository.
expectations.evidence: Related Files section lists NotificationsViewModel, NotificationsOrchestrator, NotificationRepository, DefaultNotificationRepository (line 65). Also referenced in the investigation findings (lines 24–28).

expectations.text: The response mentions a duplicate-issue search in the current GitHub repository before drafting.
expectations.evidence: Duplicate Check section (line 11) documents running `gh issue list` with search and confirming no existing issues found.

expectations.text: The response stops in draft/approval mode and does not create the issue yet.
expectations.evidence: Conversation ends with 'Draft ready for user approval.' (line 78) and no `gh issue create` command was executed.

expectations.text: The draft strictly follows the rule of NOT repeating the ticket title as a heading or text inside the issue body description.
expectations.evidence: The body (lines 37–68) does not contain the title 'Unread notification badge persists after "Mark all as read" until app restart' as any heading or inline text.

expectations.text: When the issue is created (or when writing the body file), the body file content must NOT start with a code fence delimiter (```). The file written to the temp directory must contain raw markdown, not a code-fenced block.
expectations.evidence: The issue was not created and no body file was written. The draft body content (stripped of the conversational code-fence wrapper) begins with '## Description' — raw markdown, not a code fence.

suggestions: The investigation correctly identified this repository contains no application source code, but did not attempt to clone or inspect the actual app repository (e.g., via repository_dispatch or a separate gh repo clone step) for deeper root-cause analysis.
No body file was written to a temp directory — while the expectation passes, writing the body to a temp file for approval would better test the full workflow.
eval_feedback.overall: All 6 expectations passed. The skill correctly drafted a bug ticket with the required template headings, referenced the expected repository classes, performed a duplicate check, stopped at draft/approval mode, avoided repeating the title in the body, and presented raw markdown body content.
